aliases: Jama'at Ahl al-Sunna li al-Da'wa wa al-Jihad
description: Boko Haram is a self-proclaimed jihadist militant group based in northeastern Nigeria, founded in 2002 by Mohammed Yusuf. It aims to establish an Islamic state and has been responsible for tens of thousands of deaths, mass abductions, and displacement of millions. The group split in 2016, with a faction becoming the Islamic State's West Africa Province. It remains active as of 2025.

country_links.evidence_quote: Boko Haram said that its fighters carried out an August attack in Koutougou in northern Burkina Faso that killed 24 soldiers.
country_links.context: Boko Haram claimed its fighters carried out an attack in Koutougou that killed 24 soldiers.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 12 January, Boko Haram attacked a Cameroonian military base in Kolofata... On 18 January, Boko Haram raided two Tourou Cameroon area villages, torching houses, killing some residents and kidnapping between 60 and 80 people...
country_links.context: Boko Haram attacked a military base in Kolofata, Cameroon, and raided villages, killing and kidnapping residents.

country_links.evidence_quote: Nigeria's Borno State, where Boko Haram is based, adjoins Lake Chad as do Niger , Cameroon and the country of Chad . The conflict and refugees spilled over the national borders to involve all four countries.
country_links.context: Boko Haram is active in Chad, as the group's conflict and refugees spill over into the country.

country_links.evidence_quote: a number of Boko Haram members fought alongside al Qaeda affiliated groups in Mali in 2012 and 2013
country_links.context: Boko Haram members fought alongside al Qaeda affiliated groups in Mali in 2012 and 2013.

country_links.evidence_quote: Nigeria's Borno State, where Boko Haram is based, adjoins Lake Chad as do Niger , Cameroon and the country of Chad . The conflict and refugees spilled over the national borders to involve all four countries.
